commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a shared legacy `openai-codex` migration plan and threads it through doctor provider/config-route, session-route, auth-config/store, and preview-warning repairs.

PR surface: Source +582, Tests +338. Total +920 across 9 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-level reproduction is high confidence: current main uses canonical `openai` for OpenAI/Codex context lookup, so legacy `openai-codex` context metadata can be ignored after provider unification. I did not run a live packaged upgrade lane in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor-supplied current-head real behavior proof and maintainer compatibility review; automation cannot recreate the contributor's claimed Windows upgrade setup proof for them.

target.title: fix(doctor): gate legacy Codex canonicalization on a migration plan
target.body:
## Summary

This PR replaces the closed legacy Codex/OpenAI doctor cleanup attempts with one shared migration-plan contract.

- Problem: `openclaw doctor --fix` currently has several partially separate decisions for legacy `openai-codex` provider removal, config route repair, session route repair, and preview warnings. Maintainer review on #90451/#92839 showed that local fixes can still lose credentials, runtime pins, wildcard intent, or explicit auth-order semantics.
- Intended outcome: doctor first builds one `openai-codex` migration plan, classifies each legacy provider/model route as `migrated` or `retained`, and then every repair surface consumes that same plan.
- Success looks like: safe legacy routes are canonicalized to `openai/*`; unsafe routes keep their legacy provider/auth/runtime/session state and emit a warning that manual migration is required.
- Out of scope: runtime fallback/shims, new canonical `openai-codex` routes, changing OpenAI/Codex defaults, or changing OAuth/provider runtime behavior outside doctor/update repair.
- Reviewer focus: whether the `migrated` / `retained` contract draws the right safety boundary for credentials, provider defaults, wildcard model maps, explicit empty OpenAI auth order, config route repair, and session-store repair.

## Linked context

Closes #90448

Related #90451
Related #92839

This is the fresh replacement PR after the #90451/#92839 closeout. It keeps the doctor/update direction maintainers preferred, but makes provider cleanup, config route repair, session route repair, and preview warnings consume the same migration plan instead of making separate local decisions.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: legacy `openai-codex` routes should only be rewritten to canonical `openai` when doctor can do so without dropping credentials, provider defaults, auth ordering intent, runtime pins, wildcard route intent, or session overrides. Unsafe legacy routes should be retained with an explicit warning instead of silently deleting or canonicalizing state.
- Real environment tested: Windows OpenClaw source checkout on this PR branch (`codex/codex-migration-plan-repair`, current PR head `2c02c74df109`), Node `v22.22.0`, pnpm `11.0.7`. The source checkout was built by the real `pnpm openclaw` runner, then `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes` was run against an isolated temporary config under `.artifacts/pr-93180-proof/`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```powershell
$env:OPENCLAW_HOME = Join-Path $PWD '.artifacts\pr-93180-proof\home'
$env:OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH = Join-Path $PWD '.artifacts\pr-93180-proof\openclaw.json'

# Seeded an isolated openclaw.json with canonical openai plus a legacy
# openai-codex route that overlaps the canonical model and carries provider
# defaults / explicit empty auth order.

pnpm openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):

The real `openclaw doctor --fix --non-interactive --yes` run reached the legacy config repair and printed the new retained-plan behavior:

```text
OpenClaw doctor

Legacy config keys detected
- models.providers: models.providers.openai-codex is legacy; run
  "openclaw doctor --fix" to move it to models.providers.openai.
- models.providers: openai-codex-responses is legacy; run "openclaw
  doctor --fix" to use openai-chatgpt-responses.

Doctor changes
Moved models.providers.openai-codex.api "openai-codex-responses" →
"openai-chatgpt-responses".
Retained models.providers.openai-codex because the legacy Codex
migration plan has retained routes or credentials.

Doctor warnings
- Legacy `openai-codex` migration is partially retained because some
  routes cannot be safely canonicalized.
- openai-codex/gpt-5.5: retained; overlapping model cannot receive
  provider default models.providers.openai-codex.baseUrl; overlapping
  model cannot receive provider default models.providers.openai-codex.api;
  overlapping model cannot merge route default
  models.providers.openai-codex.models[].contextWindow; overlapping model
  cannot merge route default models.providers.openai-codex.models[].maxTokens.
- Doctor will only rewrite legacy routes whose migration plan status is
  `migrated`; retained routes must be moved manually after
  credentials/runtime policy are confirmed.
```

Focused regression coverage also passed after the patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts

Test Files  3 passed
Tests       270 passed
```

Type and whitespace validation:

```text
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

- Observed result after fix: doctor no longer treats legacy provider removal and route repair as independent decisions. The live doctor output shows the overlapping legacy route is retained, the legacy API value is normalized, the provider is kept instead of deleted, and the user gets an explicit manual-migration warning. The targeted tests cover migrated routes, retained runtime/auth pins, explicit empty canonical OpenAI auth order, wildcard route maps, config route repair, session route repair, and duplicate preview warning suppression.
- What was not tested: I did not run a packaged upgrade-survivor Docker lane or mutate a production user config. The isolated CLI smoke exited after the migration proof because the temporary config intentionally lacked unrelated gateway health requirements on this Windows host (`gateway.mode`, gateway credentials, and local scheduled-task/port state). The retained-plan migration behavior above did execute and was also validated by focused doctor tests and type checking.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: this proof uses a local source checkout plus isolated temp config rather than a published npm package. The CLI output includes unrelated local host warnings from the existing Windows Gateway service; the PR behavior under review is the legacy Codex migration plan and doctor repair output shown above.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): before this planner split, the earlier local fixes could canonicalize or delete legacy `openai-codex` state while treating provider cleanup, config route repair, session route repair, and preview warnings separately. Maintainer review on #90451/#92839 identified the remaining data-loss surfaces: credentials, provider defaults, wildcard intent, runtime/session pins, and explicit auth-order semantics.

## Tests and validation

Commands run on current head `2c02c74df109`:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/commands/doctor/shared/legacy-config-migrate.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/codex-route-warnings.test.ts src/commands/doctor/shared/preview-warnings.test.ts
pnpm tsgo:test:src
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
```

Results:

```text
Vitest: 3 shards passed, 270 tests passed
tsgo:test:src: passed
git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD: passed
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Builds a shared legacy `openai-codex` migration plan with per-route `migrated` and `retained` decisions.
- Retains legacy provider/model state when credentials, provider defaults, wildcard entries, explicit empty OpenAI auth order, or overlapping canonical defaults make automatic canonicalization unsafe.
- Rewrites config routes only for entries the migration plan marks as `migrated`.
- Rewrites session routes only for entries the migration plan marks as `migrated`, preserving retained runtime/auth/session pins.
- Emits one retained-plan preview warning instead of duplicating the older blocked-merge warning.
- Adds retained wildcard model-map regression coverage.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. `openclaw doctor` / `openclaw doctor --fix` now reports and preserves unsafe legacy Codex migration state instead of silently canonicalizing or deleting it.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Doctor migration now makes config/session route repair depend on a shared legacy Codex migration plan. Safe legacy routes migrate; unsafe routes are retained for manual migration.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No runtime network/tool execution behavior changes. Auth-adjacent migration behavior changes only in the protective direction: retained legacy routes keep credential/auth/runtime state instead of being dropped or rewritten when intent is ambiguous.

What is the highest-risk area?

The migration boundary: a route marked `migrated` may be rewritten to canonical `openai`, while a route marked `retained` must not lose legacy provider/auth/runtime/session state.

How is that risk mitigated?

The new plan object is shared by provider migration, config route repair, session route repair, and preview warnings. Tests cover the migrated path, retained path, overlapping canonical models, explicit empty OpenAI auth order, wildcard model maps, runtime/auth pins, session overrides, and warning output.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review on the migration-plan contract and CI/ClawSweeper feedback.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and ClawSweeper are expected to rerun after this PR body refresh. Broader package-upgrade proof can be added if maintainers want package-level validation beyond the local source checkout and focused doctor coverage.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

This PR addresses the maintainer closeout on #90451/#92839 by replacing local preservation/canonicalization fixes with one shared plan consumed across provider migration, config route repair, session route repair, and preview warnings.
target.author: ooiuuii
